title: ConfigMaps, Secrets, Environment, Projection, Rotation, and Change Propagation · Guided Manifest Studio
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working configuration and secret propagation contract for configmaps, secrets, environment, projection, rotation, and change propagation. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of configuration and secret propagation contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create and consume ConfigMaps through keys, files, env, envFrom, command arguments, immutable objects, and size or naming boundaries.
Distinguish base64 representation, API and etcd encryption, transport protection, RBAC, node exposure, application use, and external secret authority.
Compose Secret, ConfigMap, service account token, downward API, and cluster trust bundle projections with scoped paths, modes, audiences, and refresh behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create and consume configmaps through keys, files, env, envfrom, command arguments, immutable objects, and size or naming boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption, keep K8s Configmap Source Consumption connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Configmap Source Consumption condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Workloads, Configuration, Probes, and Autoscaling — Controls Pods, init and sidecar containers, Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, configuration, lifecycle, probes, resources, QoS, disruption, and HPA.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Configmap Source Consumption precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop release embeds a credential in Git, mixes immutable and rotating config, uses env projection where a file watch is required, and grants broad Secret reads. Separate creation, consumption, update propagation, encryption, RBAC, and rotation evidence.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Configmap Source Consumption, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Every ConfigMap update immediately and atomically changes all container environment variables.

options.text: Every ConfigMap update immediately and atomically changes all container environment variables. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for K8s Configmap Source Consumption.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof. State the K8s Configmap Source Consumption preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Secret Rotation Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-secret-rotation-boundary-17dbun". Tailor its config-secret-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Secret Rotation Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Secret Rotation Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Secret Rotation Boundary, keep K8s Secret Rotation Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-secret-rotation-boundary-17dbun
  - competency: k8s-secret-rotation-boundary
    artifact: config-secret-policy.yaml
    decision: secret rotation boundary
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drain a node while the disruption budget has no headroom
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 17dbun
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-configmap
      runtime-secret: projected-bound-source
      token-audience: intake-api
      environment-refresh: rollout-required
      mounted-refresh: observed
      redaction: enforced
    verify: kubectl get configmap status-config -o json && kubectl auth can-i get secrets --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api
    failure: Reject base64-as-encryption, static environment rotation, broad secret reads, subPath refresh claims, or values leaked through rendered output.
    repair: Separate config and secret authority, scope projections and audiences, trigger observable rollouts, rotate credentials, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Secret Rotation Boundary structural check is only one gate. configuration and secret propagation contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Competency probe: Design short-lived credentials, audience, rotation, revocation, redaction, audit, reload, failure, and recovery evidence across workload and external systems.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
